reforms.description: The UK government has introduced and extended a special treatment of R&D tax credits aimed at enhancing R&D activities among small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and larger companies, incentivizing innovation and investment in research capabilities across a broader range of firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The extension of R&D tax credits is expected to stimulate innovation and increase R&D investment, thereby enhancing productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been actively applied to enhance R&D activities among SMEs and larger firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in the R&D funding landscape, impacting a wide range of firms.
reforms.source_quote: Recent measures include the creation of a research and development (R&D) tax credit for small and medium-sized enterprises, which is to be extended to large firms.

reforms.description: The UK government has announced initiatives to enhance the involvement of the private sector in funding and operating public capital stock through public-private partnerships, alongside the launch of the Small Business Research Initiative and improvements to the Smart scheme, which provide grants for innovation projects by SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives are designed to increase R&D investment and operational efficiency, fostering innovation and competitiveness in the SME sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has publicly stated intentions to implement these initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, these reforms are narrower in scope compared to major structural changes.
reforms.source_quote: The Government intends to expand the involvement of the private sector in the funding and operation of the public capital stock where this is expected to yield gains in operational efficiency.

reforms.description: The IT New Deal was launched as a government-business partnership to enhance skills in the IT sector, targeting long-term unemployed and lone parents with training and job opportunities in collaboration with major tech companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing skills in the IT sector, the initiative aims to improve employability and support the growth of innovative firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been actively implemented to provide training and job opportunities.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to address skills gaps in the IT sector, though it is not a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The IT New Deal encompasses three programmes aimed at helping the long-term unemployed and lone parents to become technicians in the IT industry.

reforms.description: The reform of the Insolvency Law aims to distinguish between reckless managers and others, which is intended to support entrepreneurship by reducing the fear of failure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing the fear of failure, the reform encourages entrepreneurial activity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been enacted to support entrepreneurship.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant for supporting entrepreneurship but does not represent a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: Innovation and entrepreneurship should not be impeded by fear of failure. The reform of the Insolvency Law, by distinguishing reckless managers from others, goes in the right direction.
